hints.bold:
Win 5 rounds to win the match.

In the tutorial, you start with 2 wins.

Breath is the energy used to play cards.

You start with 1 breath and gain 1 more each round.
Mouse over each number and keyword on a card to learn more.

Dove costs 1 breath to play and earns 1 point.

Mouse over its keywords to learn more.
Dash is worth 3 points, minus 1 for each card that was played before it.
Mercy draws cards when it resolves, not when it's played.

Start each match with 3 cards in hand.

Your max hand size is 6.
When you're ready, click a card in your hand to add it to the story.

I play a Dove, too.

You can see it in the story because it's Visible.

You can't afford to play any more cards, so you pass.

The round ends now, and the cards we've played resolve from left to right.

A new round begins.

We each draw 2 new cards.

Our max breath goes up by 1, and refills completely.
Playing Dash here earns 3 points - surely beating whatever I play...

night.bold:
We both earned 1 point, so it's a tie!

Click the moon when you're ready for the next round to begin.

Another round ends.

Click the moon to continue.
Card effects happen when the card resolve (At night), not when it's played.

hints.bold:
If you play a card with Inspire, you will have extra breath on the next round.

Play Stars now so you can afford Mercy next round.

This round you're Inspired for 1 extra breath.

Inspired and other statuses appear above your hand.

With your 3 breath you can afford Mercy.

But if you gained extra breath from Stars, you might have nothing to spend it on next round...
Sometimes it's better to pass instead of playing a card. Click the sun to pass.
With just a Dove, you probably can't win this round. But if you play enough Stars you can play Uprising next round to win for sure!
You draw cards from your deck. Your deck starts with 15 cards in it. Click to see everything in your deck.
When cards resolve or are discarded, they go to your discard pile.
If your deck is empty when you would draw a card, your discard pile is shuffled to form a new deck.
Nourish will make the next card you resolve worth extra points, even if that card is played on a future round. Use it carefully to get points when you need them.
From here on it's up to you, show me what you've learned!
